demographics.living_situation: lives at home; adult child is present and helping make the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: It felt like someone ripped my chest open from the inside, and the pain shot straight up into my jaw.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came on all of a sudden about three-quarters of an hour ago while I was lifting a heavy box, I blacked out for a moment, and now I feel terrible — weak, breathless and drenched in sweat.

symptom_narrative.onset: very sudden, while lifting a heavy box about 45 minutes ago — it hit full force within a few seconds
symptom_narrative.duration: about 45 minutes and it hasn't let up
symptom_narrative.character: a tearing, ripping feeling deep in the chest, like being torn from the inside
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst pain I have ever felt — a 10 out of 10, I couldn't even stay standing
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: in the front of my chest, and it shot straight up into my neck and jaw
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started; it reached its worst almost instantly rather than building up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any movement seems to make me feel worse, so I'm trying to stay still
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I blacked out for a few seconds right after the pain started; since I came round I feel very weak, lightheaded, short of breath and I can't stop sweating
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: never had anything like this before — I've never had angina or chest pain
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm slumped in the chair clutching my chest and I can barely move or talk

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: high blood pressure for years — I've been told it's not well controlled
past_medical_history.how_managed: a blood pressure tablet, but I don't take it reliably

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home when it happened; adult child is there and assisting with the call

objective_data_offered.value: 185/100 mmHg on the home cuff on the right arm
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on a home cuff during severe distress; may be inaccurate

objective_data_offered.value: 145/85 mmHg on the same home cuff on the left arm
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on the same cuff moments later; arm-to-arm differences on home cuffs can be technique-related and need proper measurement to confirm

objective_data_offered.value: about 110 beats per minute on a smartwatch
he looks grey and pale, is drenched in sweat, and can't get comfortable in the chair
